visual_features.overall_style: Dark, edgy, minimalist layout with strong contrast and a slightly grunge/indie aesthetic suitable for a music band. The design mixes a clean, grid-based structure with a textured black background and a hand-drawn logo, giving it both professional and underground vibes.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, contrast, and position. The full-width black header with the white band logo is the first focal point. The large, light-grey 'ARCHIVE' page title on the white band below becomes the next strong anchor due to scale and contrast. Below, the large post artwork image on the left dominates the content area, while the right sidebar with smaller text (Search, Recent Posts, Category) is visually secondary. All-caps section titles and navigation labels further emphasize key elements.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is used generously, especially in the white page-title band, which has large vertical padding above and below the breadcrumb and 'ARCHIVE' heading. In the main dark content area, there is substantial padding around the centered content container and adequate spacing between sidebar modules. The layout feels spacious rather than cramped, with clear separation between header, page title, and content.
unique_elements: Hand-drawn, angular band logo type in the top-left of the header
Full-width, high-contrast black header bar paired with a stark white secondary band for page titles
Subtle textured/noise dark background in the main content area rather than a flat color
Icon + label horizontal navigation (small white icons preceding each nav item)
Minimalist underline-only search field with right-aligned search icon

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Likely a centered, fixed-width container on a 12-column grid, with a two-column content layout (wide main column + narrower sidebar).
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width header, followed by a full-width page-title band, then a centered two-column content area on a dark background with a right sidebar.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-to-bottom structure: (1) full-width black site header with logo and horizontal navigation, (2) full-width white band containing breadcrumb and large page title ('ARCHIVE'), (3) main content section on a dark textured background with a left main content column (post artwork/content) and a right sidebar (search, recent posts, categories).
layout_characteristics.alignment: Content is globally center-aligned within a max-width container. Within sections, text is left-aligned; navigation items are right-aligned within the header. Sidebar content is right column aligned but text inside remains left-aligned. Vertical stacking is consistent and aligned to a baseline grid.
responsive_hints: Centered fixed-width content container suggests breakpoints where container width would adjust for smaller screens.
Two-column layout (main + sidebar) will likely collapse to a single column on mobile with the sidebar moving below the main content.
Navigation icons plus short labels imply potential stacking or conversion to a compact menu (e.g., hamburger) on smaller viewports.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: full-width header, followed by a full-width white page-title band. Below that, a centered main content container with a two-column layout: the left column holds large post artwork (likely followed by post metadata and text further down), while the right column acts as a sidebar containing vertically stacked modules: a search field at the top, then 'Recent Posts', then 'Category' and other widgets. Each sidebar module is separated by vertical spacing and clear headings.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header occupies a relatively small vertical portion but spans full width. The white title band beneath the header takes up a notable vertical slice (roughly 15–20% of the observed viewport height) with abundant empty space around the page title text. The main content area dominates the remainder of the viewport: the left main column visually takes about 65–70% of the content width, while the right sidebar occupies about 30–35%. Within the main column, the post artwork image is large and prominent, nearly filling the column width. Sidebar modules are compact but well-spaced, with small text blocks.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious layout. The header and page title area are very spacious with large padding and minimal elements. The main content area is more content-dense due to the large image and sidebar widgets, but there is still clear padding between content blocks and sufficient breathing room, avoiding a cluttered feel.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top-left with the distinctive band logo, then scans to the right along the navigation bar (left-to-right top horizontal flow). It then drops vertically to the high-contrast 'ARCHIVE' heading in the white band, and from there moves down to the main large artwork image in the left column. After the main image is taken in, attention shifts to the right sidebar, starting with the search field at the top and then scanning down through 'RECENT POSTS' and 'CATEGORY' headings. Overall this creates a classic F-pattern: top horizontal scan (logo + nav), then down the left main content column, with secondary horizontal attention to the sidebar modules.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit standalone buttons are clearly visible in the screenshot, but navigation items act as text-buttons. They appear as uppercase labels with small icons, white on a black background, with no visible borders and likely minimal or no corner rounding (pure text, flat style). Any primary CTA buttons in this system would likely follow a flat style: sharp or slightly rounded (2–4px radius) rectangles, solid fill (white on black or black on white), with no heavy shadows, relying on contrast and typography rather than skeuomorphic effects.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Buttons are likely implemented either as <a> elements styled as buttons (for navigation and links) or native <button> elements for forms, wrapped in simple containers for layout. Navigation items likely use a <ul> with <li><a> pairs, each containing an icon element (e.g., <span> or <i>) followed by text.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The main post appears as a large image block sitting on the dark textured background with no visible card border, no shadow, and no bounding box other than the image itself. If other cards exist, they likely follow a minimal style: content blocks separated by spacing rather than heavy borders, with maybe a thin 1px border (#222–#333) or subtle shadow for emphasis. Corners appear sharp (0px radius). Internal padding around text and images is moderate, likely 16–24px when contained.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical card structure would be a container <article> or <div> representing a post, containing an <a> wrapping the featured image, followed by a text block with title, meta, and excerpt. Separation between cards is via margin-bottom rather than distinct panels.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Search form uses a minimalist underlined input. The text field has no visible box; instead, a thin horizontal line (1px) forms the bottom border in a light grey/white tone. Placeholder text 'Search...' appears in small, white or light grey text, left-aligned. On the right end of the line sits a small black-on-white or white-on-black search icon (magnifying glass) providing an interaction cue. The overall form density is low, with ample horizontal space around the field and vertical spacing separating it from other sidebar sections.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Form is likely structured as a simple <form> with a single <input type="search"/> and either a background-positioned icon or an <button type="submit"> containing the magnifying-glass icon, positioned with flexbox. Labels are implicit (placeholder text in the input) rather than external labels. Inputs are probably full-width within sidebar modules.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal menu in the top black header. Items are all-caps labels like FRONT, MUSIC, VIDEOS, LIVE, ABOUT, each preceded by a small white icon (home, circle, play, lightning, etc.). Text is white on black, likely using a condensed sans-serif. Items are spaced evenly with consistent horizontal padding, with no visible separators or background highlights in the default state. Active or hover states are not visible but are likely indicated through subtle changes such as text color shift (to a slightly grey or off-white), underline, or icon opacity change.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Navigation likely uses a semantic <nav> element containing a <ul> list of <li> items, each with an <a>. Icons may be defined via an icon font (<i>) or inline SVGs before the link text. Active page may be represented with an 'active' class applied to the corresponding <li> or <a> for styling.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar lists (Recent Posts, Category) are very minimal. Each section is headed by an all-caps label in a slightly bolder or larger grey/white font. List items, when present, would likely appear as single-line or two-line text entries with tight vertical spacing (8–12px) and no bullets, separated only by whitespace. No visible horizontal rules or strong borders; emphasis is on typography and spacing.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Lists are probably simple <ul>/<li> structures for recent posts and categories. Items might be plain text links (<a>) styled with no underline in default state and underline or color change on hover. Layout is vertical with full-width clickable areas inside the sidebar column.

heading.visual_style: Condensed, all-caps sans-serif with relatively narrow width, straight strokes, and a slightly industrial feel. Used for navigation labels and section headings like 'ARCHIVE' and 'RECENT POSTS'.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif with good legibility, regular proportions, and minimal personality, supporting the dark, content-focused design without drawing attention away from imagery and headings.

spacing_system.component_padding: Common internal padding for text blocks and sidebar modules is approximately 16–24px on all sides, with tighter 8–12px padding for compact items like nav links.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header, title band, main content) appears around 32–48px, while spacing between smaller components (e.g., sidebar modules) is around 16–24px.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely show hover feedback via text color change (e.g., from #FFFFFF to a softer grey) and possibly underline or subtle icon brightness shift.
Text links in sidebar lists probably underline or change color on hover to reinforce clickability.
Search input underline may change color or thickness on focus/hover, and the search icon might darken/lighten or animate slightly.
Image thumbnails (post artwork) likely have a hover effect such as slight brightness increase, overlay, or subtle zoom to suggest interactivity.
Transitions, if present, are probably short (150–250ms) ease-in-out opacity or transform transitions, consistent with the minimal, modern aesthetic.
